title: salix × phaeophylla
intro: Salix × phaeophylla, also known as the hybrid willow, is a cross between the black willow (Salix nigra) and the Sitka willow (Salix sitchensis), resulting in a unique plant species. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, and is used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Salix × phaeophylla include relief from pain, inflammation, and fever, making it a natural remedy for conditions such as arthritis and headaches. The herbal preparations of Salix × phaeophylla include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the bark in hot water or a solvent like ethanol, allowing for the extraction of its medicinal compounds.
